key_departures.impact: 7-year Texans veteran and versatile starter at guard/tackle was traded to Cleveland for a 2026 fifth-round pick. Howard was entering the final year of his deal and reportedly wanted to play right tackle exclusively. His departure removes a key, experienced piece from a chronically troubled OL.

Former second-round pick sent to Detroit as part of the David Montgomery trade (with a 2026 4th and 2027 7th). Scruggs started just one game in 2025 after failing to live up to draft status. While not a major loss on the field, it depletes IOL depth.

Cut before the new league year, clearing $8M in cap space. Mixon missed all of 2025 with a foot injury. The Texans quickly replaced him via the David Montgomery trade. Departure was expected and proactively addressed.

Lost to Washington on a 3yr/$24M deal (up to $25.5M). Settle had been a full-time starter on Houston's elite defense, logging 29 starts over two years with the Texans. His departure creates a genuine void at starting IDL behind Rankins.
Fourth-year Texan was phased out of the defensive rotation during 2025, becoming primarily a special teams player. His departure is a minor roster move, not a significant need creator.

Acquired from Jacksonville in the 2025 offseason, Kirk never consistently claimed a starting role in Houston. With Jaylin Noel emerging and Tank Dell expected back healthy, Kirk was considered one-and-done. Depth loss only.

Signed to a one-year deal in summer 2025, Chubb gradually faded down the depth chart behind Woody Marks. The Montgomery trade made his departure a foregone conclusion. RB need was simultaneously created and filled.
Missed substantial time in 2025 with injuries, recording just 3.5 sacks in 12 games at age 35. Expected departure; Logan Hall was signed specifically as a replacement-caliber piece.
Two-year Texans punter departed as a free agent. Texans proactively addressed the void by trading for Kai Kroeger from New Orleans.

signings.analysis: One-year, $40.1M fully-guaranteed extension (all $40.1M secured at signing) keeps the 15-sack, 2nd-team All-Pro locked in through 2027. The deal makes Hunter one of the top-paid defensive ends in the NFL. While it's only one year — signaling the team isn't fully locked in long-term — the full guarantee on a single season is an extraordinary commitment. EDGE was not a need before FA and remains a strength. The 1-year structure means they'll revisit again next offseason, but for 2026 purposes this is done.

Three-year, $38.6M re-sign with $23.5M guaranteed locks in the starting right guard through 2028. Ingram had his best season in 2025 as a run blocker but struggled in pass protection. At $12.9M AAV with multi-year guarantees, this is a real investment — not a bridge. Retaining Ingram partially addresses the IOL need (right guard is now set), but center remains a glaring hole after losing out on top FA options. IOL need drops from critical to moderate.

Two-year re-sign with $12M fully guaranteed keeps Rankins — who started all 17 games in 2025 — anchoring the interior. At $7M AAV, this is slightly below mid-market for a starting IDL, but the guarantees signal genuine commitment for a player who turns 32. However, with Tim Settle Jr. departing to Washington, Rankins is now the sole reliable IDL starter. Houston still needs IDL depth/rotation. Need partially addressed — Rankins holds down one spot but a second starter-caliber IDL is needed.

Traded for Montgomery (2 remaining years, base salaries of $5.49M in 2026 and $7.49M in 2027, no guaranteed money remaining) to address the void left by Joe Mixon's release. Montgomery had 2,506 rushing yards and 33 touchdowns in 3 seasons with Detroit and should immediately serve as the RB1 in a committee with Woody Marks. The lack of guaranteed money suggests Houston will aim to extend him. RB need is fully addressed — Montgomery plus Marks is a legitimate 1-2 punch.

Three-year, $24.75M deal with $16.5M guaranteed brings in a 25-year-old starting safety who had 4 interceptions in 2024. He slots in alongside Calen Bullock as a legitimate starter in a secondary that already features Derek Stingley Jr. and Kamari Lassiter. At $8.25M AAV, he is at the lower end of starter-tier money for a safety, representing solid value. The 3-year structure with significant guarantees signals a real commitment. Safety need is fully addressed.

Two-year, $25M deal ($13.5M guaranteed) brings in a 29-year-old tackle who started 105 of 107 career games with Indianapolis — mostly at right tackle. Smith provides immediate starting-caliber experience and versatility (was originally drafted as a guard). At $12.5M AAV, this is mid-tier OT money, appropriate for a quality-not-elite tackle. Two years with meaningful guarantees is real commitment, though the shorter duration signals the Texans still want to find a longer-term answer. After trading Tytus Howard, OT goes from critical to moderate need. Still a priority position in the draft.

One-year, $7M re-sign keeps the veteran insurance tackle back for 2026. Brown was the team's best lineman when healthy in 2025, but has missed significant time in multiple recent seasons and played only 7 games as a starter before an ankle injury ended his season. As a 1-year deal at age 33 with a severe injury history, this is a depth/bridge signing, not a starter solution. Does not move the OT needle meaningfully — Braden Smith is the primary OT addition.

Two-year, $13M deal ($7.5M guaranteed) re-signs a linebacker who started 9 games in his first year in Houston. Speed is a quality rotational piece who can start if needed. At $6.5M AAV with two years of guarantees, this is a legitimate commitment to the LB room. With Christian Harris departing, Speed takes on a more prominent role. LB depth is addressed but Azeez Al-Shaair's extension looms as the real priority at this position.

One-year, $12.6M extension retains Schultz, who set a career high with 82 catches in 2025. At $12.6M AAV, this is squarely in the starter tier for TE. The 1-year structure is a bridge deal — Schultz is 29 and Houston will revisit again next offseason — but the position is covered for 2026. TE was not a need heading into FA, and it remains stable.

One-year, up-to-$4M deal brings in a 26-year-old depth edge rusher from Chicago. Robinson was a rotational piece for the Bears and provides developmental/rotation value behind Hunter and Anderson. Low-cost depth addition, does not move any need needle. EDGE depth improves marginally.

Two-year, ~$14M deal (reported ~$7M/year) brings back a Houston-area product who was Tampa's 33rd overall pick in 2022. Hall offers edge/interior versatility at 6'6", 283 lbs and functions as a direct replacement for Denico Autry and partial replacement for Tim Settle. He started a career-high 16 games in 2025 but had only 1.5 sacks. The 2-year structure with ~$7M guaranteed signals real commitment, though he's more rotation than starter. Combined with Rankins re-sign, IDL need is partially addressed but not fully resolved — a starter-caliber IDL partner for Rankins remains a draft priority.

One-year, $2M re-sign (up to $2.5M with incentives) keeps a veteran IDL depth piece in the building. Jones is a run-stuffing reserve who never gets above 40% defensive snaps. Depth retention only — does not address the starter-level IDL vacancy left by Settle.
Two-year, $13M extension makes Fairbairn the highest-paid kicker in the NFL. This was a retention of the incumbent; K was not a need before FA and remains solved.

Low-stakes punter trade (2028 6th for 2028 7th + Kroeger) replaces Tommy Townsend with a 23-year-old on his rookie deal through 2027. Kroeger averaged 44.8 yards per punt as a 2025 Saints rookie. Economical solution to the punter vacancy. Not a positional need item.

One-year, $3M re-sign of the versatile safety who missed the second half of 2025 with a quad injury. Depth/insurance signing for the secondary. Does not change the S need picture — Blankenship is the starter-level addition.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Joe Mixon cut, Nick Chubb departed, but Houston proactively traded for David Montgomery (2 yrs remaining) to pair with Woody Marks. Montgomery had 33 TDs in 3 Lions seasons. This is a legitimate 1-2 punch. RB is no longer a meaningful draft need.

Reed Blankenship signed to a 3yr/$24.75M deal ($16.5M gtd) provides a proven starter alongside Calen Bullock. M.J. Stewart re-signed as depth. Safety is resolved for multiple years. No longer a draft priority.

Danielle Hunter extended (1yr/$40.1M fully guaranteed), Will Anderson Jr. extension imminent. Both are top-5 edge rushers. Dominique Robinson and Logan Hall add rotation depth behind them. Denico Autry's departure is negligible given the elite starters in place. EDGE is a strength, not a need.

Ed Ingram re-signed (3yr/$38.6M, $23.5M gtd) locks in the starting RG. However, center remains a critical vacancy — top FA centers (Linderbaum, Cade Mays) signed elsewhere. Jake Andrews returns but is not a reliable starter. IOL drops from critical but center is still a genuine need and a top draft target in rounds 1-3.

Tytus Howard was traded away, creating a hole. Braden Smith signed (2yr/$25M, $13.5M gtd) as the primary RT solution and brings 105 career starts but has missed significant time in recent seasons. Trent Brown re-signed (1yr/$7M) as the backup/bridge. OT is partially addressed but still a meaningful draft need — Houston likely targets OT on Day 1 or 2 of the draft given Brown's injury history.

Sheldon Rankins re-signed (2yr/$14M, $12M gtd) and Logan Hall added (2yr/$14M). Naquan Jones back on minimum. BUT Tim Settle Jr. departed to Washington on a 3yr/$24M deal, leaving a genuine hole next to Rankins. Net: IDL depth improved, but starter-level IDL remains a priority. Need tier holds at 2 — should draft IDL in rounds 1-3.

E.J. Speed re-signed (2yr/$13M) as a viable rotational/starting LB. However, Azeez Al-Shaair's extension is still pending — he is entering the final year of his deal and could become very expensive on the open market if not extended. Christian Harris departed (depth loss only). LB need stays at tier 2 pending Al-Shaair resolution; if he signs an extension this drops to tier 3-4.

Christian Kirk departed (rotational loss only). Tank Dell expected back from injury, Jaylin Noel emerging in year 2, Nico Collins locked in. No WR additions made in FA. Need unchanged — Houston could still use a WR2/slot opposite Nico Collins in the mid-rounds of the draft.
C.J. Stroud is the unquestioned starter. No change. Extension discussions ongoing.

key_additions.headline: Power RB1 acquired via trade (for Scruggs + 4th + 7th) to replace released Joe Mixon — 33 TDs in 3 Lions seasons
Historic 1-year, $40.1M fully-guaranteed extension keeps 2nd-team All-Pro (15 sacks) locked in through 2027
Starting RG locked in on multi-year deal, preventing an IOL catastrophe after losing Tytus Howard and Juice Scruggs
Young, proven starter (3yr/$24.75M) fills the long-vacant safety2 slot alongside Calen Bullock
Veteran OT with 105 career starts steps in as the primary right tackle replacement for traded Tytus Howard
Full-time IDL starter retained on veteran-friendly deal after anchoring Houston's elite 2025 defense
UH hometown product and former 2nd-round pick adds versatile IDL/EDGE rotation depth behind elite pass-rush duo
Linebacker who started 9 games in his first Texans season retained on a real multi-year commitment
Career-high 82-catch TE keeps the position stable on a bridge deal for 2026

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Browns (3yr/$63M extension with Cleveland), HOU received 2026 5th-round pick
key_losses.headline: Seven-year franchise stalwart dealt to Cleveland, creating urgent OT/IOL overhaul heading into 2026

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Lions with 2026 4th + 2027 7th for RB David Montgomery
key_losses.headline: Second-round bust departure costs Houston draft capital but yields a legitimate RB1 in Montgomery
Starting DT cashed in with Washington, leaving a hole next to Rankins on Houston's elite defensive front
Veteran RB cut after missing all of 2025 with a foot injury; Montgomery trade was the immediate answer
One-year rental WR departs as Tank Dell's return and Jaylin Noel's emergence reduce the need for his slot
Two-year Texans punter replaced economically via Kai Kroeger trade from New Orleans

notes: Contract for Ed Ingram reported at both $37.5M (Rapoport/NFL Network) and $38.6M (KPRC2/click2houston) — using $38.6M per KPRC2 Aaron Wilson sourcing. Sheldon Rankins total contract value not explicitly confirmed beyond $12M guaranteed on a 2-year deal; total value estimated at $14M based on $7M AAV (up from $5.25M prior deal). Logan Hall guarantees reported only as 'much of the deal will be guaranteed' per agent; estimated at ~$7M (50% of total). Azeez Al-Shaair extension has mutual interest reported but no deal confirmed as of March 11, 2026 — not included. Will Anderson Jr. extension is an offseason priority but unconfirmed — not included. David Montgomery contract remaining value sourced from Spotrac ($5.49M 2026 base + $7.49M 2027 base = ~$12.98M, no guarantees remaining). Trent Brown guarantees not officially reported; estimated at ~50% of AAV.
